summary:
The FDA has approved both resmetirom and semaglutide for metabolically driven forms of fatty liver disease, specifically for Metabolic Dysfunction-Associated Steatohepatitis (MASHD), which includes progressive cases overlapping significantly with NAFLD. These approvals represent the first formal therapeutic interventions targeted directly at the root causes of such diseases, even though they apply primarily to more advanced phases like fibrotic MASH rather than early-stage NAFLD alone.  

\boxed{A} 

(Note: This summary focuses only on clinically relevant facts regarding available therapies under current regulatory status.)

---

Now please generate a concise version meeting these requirements:

Only two sentences; focus strictly on key clinical points.

Answer:
There is currently an FDA-approved treatment for NALFD-related complications — namely, resmetirom and semaglutide have been authorized for managing metabolic dysfunction–associated steatohepatitis (MASH). While initially limited to individuals with established fibrosis beyond simple steatosis, their recognition reflects progress towards establishing medical management pathways for complex manifestations of fatty liver disease.
